checks.a_analysis: The output text faithfully follows the structure and content of the source PDF. No evidence of hallucinated sections, paragraphs, or body text was found. AI-added metadata and accessibility headers are within acceptable limits according to the task definition.

The output does not contain any phone numbers, emails, or URLs. While a block of existing phone numbers from the source is missing (addressed in C5), no incorrect or fabricated contact information has been added by the pipeline.

All seven pages from the source PDF are represented in the output document. No entire sections or pages were silently dropped, although specific data blocks within pages are missing (addressed in C4 and C5).

The 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' tables appearing on pages 4 and 6 of the source document are entirely missing as structured table elements in the output. They have been converted to images (element indices 26 and 32) and marked as decorative/decorative, resulting in a 100% loss of the data rows (member names and their respective vote records) which are essential components of the meeting minutes.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' on page 4 and page 6 containing 16 data rows representing board members and their votes ('Y' or 'Not Present').
checks.b_evidence_output: [The tables are absent from the structured JSON output; represented only as images with no alt text at indices 26 and 32]

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: A distinct block of contact information containing a list of 12 phone numbers on page 3 of the source document is completely omitted from the output. This information appears between the 'GUESTS' table and the 'Chair’s Opening Remarks' section in the source but is missing entirely from the conversion result.
checks.b_evidence_output: [The entire block of phone numbers is missing from the element list between index 9 and 10]

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted shows high fidelity to the source. All captured financial figures (e.g., PSAP Fund Balance $14,348,738), dates, and attendee names match the source document accurately.

checks.a_analysis: The output contains two identical images (image-2 and image-3) representing the 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table. However, upon inspection of the source document, this table appears twice in the original PDF: once on page 4 (following the consent agenda) and once on page 6 (preceding the Finance Team Report). Since the duplication exists in the source, its presence in the output is not a pipeline defect.

The document contains a single heading element (H2) for the title. While the section headers (1, 2, 3, etc.) are rendered as bold paragraphs rather than headings, there is no skipping of levels or incorrect nesting within the heading elements themselves. The normalization of the title to H2 is an acceptable change.

The 'ROLL CALL VOTE' tables appearing on page 4 and page 6 of the source document are converted as static images (image-2 and image-3) rather than structured table elements. This represents a failure to preserve the document's table structure, as the data within these tables is not accessible as rows or columns.
checks.b_evidence_source: NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE Friday, May 22, 2020 (Table structure with columns for Consent Agenda and PSAP Distribution)

checks.a_analysis: A significant block of text containing a list of twelve phone numbers located on page 3 (between the Guests roll call table and Section 1) is entirely missing from the JSON output. This omission breaks the logical reading flow intended by the source document.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text does not appear in the JSON elements between table-4 and paragraph-5.

checks.a_analysis: The numbered sections and their sub-items (e.g., Section 5 a-f, Section 6 c-g) are correctly identified and organized as list elements or clear paragraphs. Nesting for item 5f/f i-ii is properly handled.
The source document contains no active URLs or email addresses. No links were fabricated in the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The multi-column layout of the attendee lists on the first page is preserved in a structured way that mirrors the source's intent. All subsequent text sections (1-13) and tables appear in the correct sequence. The overall visual flow and grouping of information are consistent with the original document, allowing users to find information in expected locations.

All tables from the source PDF are correctly rendered as HTML tables. This includes the initial attendee lists, the 'ROLL CALL' table, the 'GUESTS' table, and the 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table. The tables have clear row and column definitions, aligned content, and distinct headers. There is no evidence of overlapping text, misaligned columns, or tables being reduced to plain text paragraphs.

Meaningful visual elements, such as the 'NC DIT' logo within the meeting tables, are present and correctly rendered in the HTML. The red 'APPROVED BY...' watermarks seen in the PDF are not present in the HTML, which is an acceptable exclusion as they are considered decorative/watermark elements. No critical images, logos, or diagrams are missing or broken.

A thorough visual inspection of the HTML output confirms that there is no duplicated body content. Each paragraph, table, and list item appears once, following the logical order of the source PDF. Section headers appearing in the navigation/TOC area as well as the body is an expected accessibility feature and not a defect.
